reasoning: Ariarne Titmus holds the 200m freestyle long course world record (1:52.23) as of October 2025. Between late October and January, the global swimming calendar is overwhelmingly focused on short course (25m) competition, including the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and the World Aquatics Swimming Championships (25m), which take place in December. Elite swimmers do not typically taper for long course (50m) events during these months. Furthermore, world records are relatively rare and usually happen at peak summer meets (Olympics, World Championships, or national trials). While competitors like Mollie O'Callaghan or Summer McIntosh are exceptionally fast, the chances of a long course WR being broken in the November-January window are exceedingly low. The main risk is an unexpected administrative stripping of the record (e.g., doping), which is highly unlikely. Thus, the probabilities remain very high and decrease slightly over time to account for the expanding time horizon.
